channel.title: Foundation-Models on Markus Knauer
channel.description: Recent content in Foundation-Models on Markus Knauer

item.title: **CLASP:** Natural Language-Driven Robot Skill Selection and Composition
item.description:
How can a robot understand what you want — and admit when it doesn’t know how? Our framework CLASP bridges the gap between data-hungry vision-language-action models and data-efficient imitation learning by combining Vision-Language Models with Task-Parameterized Kernelized Movement Primitives (TP-KMPs), requiring only 2–5 demonstrations per skill.

CLASP operates in three stages:

item.title: **RA-L 2026:** IROSA — Interactive Robot Skill Adaptation using Natural Language
item.description:
What if you could adapt a robot’s behavior just by talking to it? Our framework IROSA enables open-vocabulary robot skill adaptation through natural language using a tool-based architecture that maintains a protective abstraction layer between the LLM and robot hardware.

The LLM selects and parameterizes validated tool functions that modify the underlying motion model, enabling speed adjustments, trajectory corrections, and obstacle avoidance — all without any fine-tuning, zero-shot using only tool descriptions.
